verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But call to remembrance the former days, in which after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions; partly, whilst ye were made a gazing stock both by reproaches and afflictions, and partly whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For ye had compassion on those who were in bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing that ye have for yourselves in heaven a better and an enduring substance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The best Physicians after they have made a deep incision, and have increased the pains by the wound, soothing the afflicted part, and giving rest and refreshment to the disturbed soul, proceed not to make a second incision, but rather soothe that which has been made with gentle remedies, and such as are suited to remove the violence of the pain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This Paul also did after he had shaken their souls, and pierced them with the recollection of Hell, and convinced then, that he must certainly perish, who does despite to the grace of God, and after he had shown from the laws of Moses, that they also shall perish, and the more [fearfully], and confirm it by other testimonies, and had said, “It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the Living God” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 31 ): then, lest the soul desponding through excessive fear, should be swallowed up with grief, he soothes them by commendations and exhortation, and gives them zeal derived from their own conduct.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, he says, “call to remembrance the former days, in which after ye had been enlightened, ye endured a great fight of afflictions.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Powerful is the exhortation from deeds [already done]: for he who begins a work ought to go forward and add to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: As if he had said, when ye were brought in [to the Church], when ye were in the rank of learners, ye displayed so great readiness, so great nobleness; but now it is no longer so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he who encourages, does thus especially encourage them from their own example.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he did not simply say, “ye endured a fight” but a “great” [fight].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover he did not say “temptations” but “fight,” which is an expression of commendation and of very great praise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he also enumerates them particularly, amplifying his discourse, and multiplying his praise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Partly” (he says) “whilst ye were made a gazing-stock by reproaches and afflictions”; for reproach is a great thing, and calculated to pervert the soul, and to darken the judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For hear what the prophet says: “While they daily say unto me, Where is thy God?” ( Ps. xlii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “If the enemy had reproached me, I would have borne it.” ( Ps. lv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For since the human race is exceedingly vainglorious, therefore it is easily overcome by this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he did not simply say “by reproaches,” but that even with great intensity, being “made a gazing-stock.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when a person is reproached alone, it is indeed painful, but far more so when in presence of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For tell me how great the evil was when men who had left the meanness of Judaism, and gone over, as it were, to the best course of life, and despised the customs of their fathers, were ill treated by their own people, and had no help.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I cannot say (he says) that ye suffered these things indeed and were grieved, but ye even rejoiced exceedingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this he expressed by saying, “Whilst ye became companions of them that were so used,” and he brings forward the Apostles themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not only (he means) were ye not ashamed of your own sufferings, but ye even shared with others who were suffering the same things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This too is the language of one who is encouraging them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said not, ‘Bear my afflictions, share with me,’ but respect your own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Ye had compassion on them that were in bonds.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that he is speaking concerning himself and the rest who were in prison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus ye did not account “bonds” to be bonds: but as noble wrestlers so stood ye: for not only ye needed no consolation in your own [distresses], but even became a consolation to others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And “ye took joyfully the spoiling of your goods.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: O! what “full assurance of faith”! ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then he also sets forth the motive, not only consoling them for their struggles, but also that they might not be shaken from the Faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When ye saw your property plundered (he means) ye endured; for already ye saw Him who is invisible, as visible: which was the effect of genuine faith, and ye showed it forth by your deeds themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Well then, the plundering was perhaps from the force of the plunderers, and no man could prevent it; so that as yet it is not clear, that ye endured the plundering for the faith’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (Although this too is clear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it was in your power if you chose, not to be plundered, by not believing.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But ye did what is far greater than this; the enduring such things even “with joy”; which was altogether apostolical, and worthy of those noble souls, who rejoiced when scourged.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, it says, “they departed from the presence of the council, rejoicing that they were counted worthy to suffer shame for the Name.” ( Acts v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But he that endures “with joy,” shows that he has some reward, and that the affair is no loss but a gain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover the expression “ye took” shows their willing endurance, because, he means, ye chose and accepted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Knowing” (he says) “that ye have for yourselves in heaven a better and an enduring substance”; instead of saying, firm, not perishing like this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In the next place, having praised them, he says, ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 35 ) “Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompense of reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What meanest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He did not say, ‘ye have cast it away, and recover it’: but, which tended more to strengthen them, “ye have it,” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For to recover again that which has been cast away, requires more labor: but not to lose that which is held fast does not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But to the Galatians he says the very opposite: “My children of whom I travail in birth again, till Christ be formed in you” ( Gal. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 19 ); and with reason; for they were more supine, whence they needed a sharper word; but these were more faint-hearted, so that they rather needed what was more soothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Cast not away therefore” (he says) “your confidence,” so that they were in great confidence towards God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Which hath” (he says) “great recompense of reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And when shall we receive them (some one might say)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All things on our part have been done.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore he anticipated them on their own supposition, saying in effect, If ye know that ye have in heaven a better substance, seek nothing here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For ye have need of patience,” not of any addition [to your labors], that ye may continue in the same state, that ye may not cast away what has been put into your hands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Ye need nothing else, but so to stand as ye have stood, that when ye come to the end, ye may receive the promise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 36 ) “For” (he says) “ye have need of patience, that after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Ye have need of one thing only, to bear with the delay; not that ye should fight again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Ye are at the very crown (he means); ye have borne all the combats of bonds, of afflictions; your goods have been spoiled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Henceforward ye are standing to be crowned: endure this only, the delay of the crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: O the greatness of the consolation!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is as if one should speak to an athlete who had overthrown all, and had no antagonist, and then was to be crowned, and yet endured not that time, during which the president of the games comes, and places the crown [upon him]; and he impatient, should wish to go out, and escape as though he could not bear the thirst and the heat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He then also hinting this, what does he say? ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 37 ) “Yet a little while and He that shall come will come, and will not tarry.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For lest they should say, And when will He come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He comforts them from the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For thus also when he says in another place, “Now is our salvation nearer” ( Rom. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 11 ), he comforts them because the remaining time is short.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this he says not of himself but from the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if from that time it was said, “Yet a little while, and He that shall come will come, and will not tarry,” it is plain that now He is nearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore also waiting is no small reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 38 ) “Now the just” (he says) “shall live by faith, but if any man draw back, My soul shall have no pleasure in him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is a great encouragement when one shows that they have succeeded in the whole matter and are losing it through a little indolence. ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 39 ) “But we are not of them that draw back unto perdition, but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 1, 2 ) “Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For by it the elders obtained a good report.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: O what an expression has he used, in saying, “an evidence of things not seen.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For [we say] there is “evidence,” in the case of things that are very plain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Faith then is the seeing things not plain (he means), and brings what are not seen to the same full assurance with what are seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So then neither is it possible to disbelieve in things which are seen, nor, on the other hand can there be faith unless a man be more fully assured with respect to things invisible, than he is with respect to things that are most clearly seen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For since the objects of hope seem to be unsubstantial, Faith gives them substantiality, or rather, does not give it, but is itself their substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For instance, the Resurrection has not come, nor does it exist substantially, but hope makes it substantial in our soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is [the meaning of] “the substance of things.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If therefore it is an “evidence of things not seen,” why forsooth do you wish to see them, so as to fall away from faith, and from being just?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since “the just shall live by faith,” whereas ye, if ye wish to see these things, are no longer faithful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Ye have labored (he says), ye have struggled: I too allow this, nevertheless, wait; for this is Faith: do not seek the whole “here.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: These things were indeed said to the Hebrews, but they are a general exhortation also to many of those who are here assembled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How and in what way?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: To the faint-hearted; to the mean-spirited.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when they see the wicked prospering, and themselves faring ill, they are troubled, they bear it impatiently: while they long for the chastisement, and the inflicting vengeance on others; while they wait for the rewards of their own sufferings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For yet a little time, and He that shall come will come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then say this to the slothful: Doubtless there will be punishment; doubtless He will come, henceforth the events of the Resurrection are even at the doors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whence [does] that [appear] (you say)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I do not say, from the prophets; for neither do I now speak to Christians only; but even if a heathen be here, I am perfectly confident, and bring forward my proofs, and will instruct him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How (you say)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Christ foretold many things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If those former things did not come to pass, then do not believe them; but if they all came to pass, why doubt concerning those that remain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And indeed, it were very unreasonable, nothing having come to pass, to believe the one, or when all has come to pass, to disbelieve the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But I will make the matter more plain by an example.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Christ said, that Jerusalem should be taken, and should be so taken as no city ever was before, and that it should never be raised up: and in fact this prediction came to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said, that there should be “great tribulation” ( Matt. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 21 ), and it came to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said that a grain of mustard seed is sown, so should the preaching [of the Gospel] be extended: and every day we see this running over the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said, that they who left father or mother, or brethren, or sisters, should have both fathers and mothers; And this we see fulfilled by facts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said, “in the world ye shall have tribulation, but be of good cheer, I have overcome the world” ( John xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 33 ), that is, no man shall get the better of you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this we see by the events has come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said that “the gates of hell shall not prevail against the Church” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 18 ), even though persecuted, and that no one shall quench the preaching [of the Gospel]: and the experience of events bears witness to this prediction also: and yet when He said these things, it was very hard to believe Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because all these were words, and He had not as yet given proof of the things spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that they have now become far more credible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said that “when the Gospel should have been preached among all the nations, then the end shall come” ( Matt. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ); lo! now ye have arrived at the end: for the greater part of the world hath been preached to, therefore the end is now at hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us tremble, beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what, tell me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Art thou anxious about the end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It indeed is itself near, but each man’s life and death is nearer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is said, “the days of our years are seventy years; but if [one be] in strength, fourscore years.” ( Ps. xc.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The day of judgment is near.

verses.text: Let us fear.

verses.text: 7 ; [LXX. xlviii.

verses.text: There we shall repent much, “but in death no man shall praise Him.” ( Ps. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For here [in this life] indeed, whatever we do has efficacy; but there, no longer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Tell me, if a man placed us for a little while in a flaming furnace, should we not submit to anything in order to escape, even were it necessary to part with our money, nay to undergo slavery?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How many have fallen into grievous diseases, and would gladly give up all, to be delivered from them, if the choice were offered them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If in this world then, a disease of short duration so afflicts us, what shall we do yonder, when repentance will be of no avail?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Of how many evils are we now full, without being conscious of them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We bite one another, we devour one another, in wronging, accusing, calumniating, being vexed by the credit of our neighbors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And see the difficulty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When a man wishes to undermine the reputation of a neighbor, he says, ‘Such an one said this of him; O God, forgive me, do not examine me strictly, I must give account of what I have heard.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then dost thou speak of it at all, if thou dost not believe it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou speak of it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou make it credible by much reporting?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou pass on the story which is not true?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou dost not believe it, and thou entreatest God not to call thee to strict account?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do not say it then, but keep silence, and free thyself from all fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But I know not from whence this disease has fallen upon men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We have become tattlers, nothing remains in our mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hear the exhortation of a wise man who says, “Hast thou heard a word?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let it die in thee, be bold; it will not burst thee.” ( Ecclus. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “A fool heareth a word, and travaileth, as a women in labor of a child.” ( Ecclus. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We are ready to make accusations, prepared for condemning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Even if no other evil thing had been done by us, this were sufficient to ruin us, and to carry us away to Hell, this involves us in ten thousand evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And that thou mayest know this certainly, hear what the prophet says, “Thou satest and spakest against thy brother.” ( Ps. l.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But it is not I, you say, but the other [who told me].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nay rather, it is thyself; for if thou hadst not spoken, another would not have heard: or even if he should hear it, yet thou wouldest not have been to blame for the sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We ought to shade over and conceal the failings of neighbors, but thou paradest them under a cloak of zeal for goodness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou becomest, not an accuser, but a gossip, a trifler, a fool.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: O what cleverness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Without being aware of it, thou bringest disgrace upon thyself as well as on him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And see what great evils which arise from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou provokest the wrath of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou not hear Paul saying about widows, “they not only” (these are his words) “learn to be idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, wandering about from house to house, and speaking things which they ought not.” ( 1 Tim. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that even when thou believest the things which are said against thy brother, thou oughtest not even in that case to speak of them; much less, when thou dost not believe them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But thou [forsooth] lookest to thine own interest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou fearest to be called to account by God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Fear then, lest even for thy tattling thou be called to account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For here, thou canst not say, ‘O God, call me not to account for light talking’: for the whole matter is light talking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why didst thou publish it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why didst thou increase the evil?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is sufficient to destroy us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: On this account Christ said, “Judge not, that ye be not judged.” ( Matt. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But we pay no regard to this, neither are we brought to our senses by what happened to the Pharisee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said what was true, “I am not as this Publican” ( Luke xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 11 ), he said it too in no man’s hearing; yet was he condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If he were condemned when he said what was true, and uttered it in no man’s hearing, what fearful [punishment] shall not they suffer, who like gossiping women, carry about everywhere lies which they do not even themselves believe?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What shall they not endure?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Henceforward let us set “a door and a bolt before the mouth.” ( Ecclus. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For innumerable evils have arisen from tattling; families have been ruined, friendships torn asunder, innumerable other miseries have happened.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Busy not thyself, O man, about the affairs of thy neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But thou art talkative and hast a weakness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Talk of thine own [faults] to God: thus the weakness will be no longer a weakness, but an advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Talk of thy own [faults] to thy friends, those who are thorough friends and righteous men, and in whom thou hast confidence, that so they may pray for thy sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If thou speak of the [sins] of others, thou art nowise profited, neither hast thou gained anything, but hast ruined thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If thou confessest thy own [sins] to the Lord, thou hast great reward: for one says, “I said, I will confess against myself mine iniquity to the Lord, and Thou forgavest the impiety of my heart.” ( Ps. xxxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou wish to judge?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Judge thine own [sins].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: No one will accuse thee, if thou condemn thyself: but he will accuse if thou do not condemn; he will accuse thee, unless thou convict thyself; will accuse thee of insensibility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou hast seen such an one angry, irritated, doing something else out of place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Think at once, even thou on thy own [faults]: and thus thou wilt not greatly condemn him, and wilt free thyself from the load of thy past transgressions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If we thus regulate our own conduct, if we thus manage our own life, if we condemn ourselves, we shall probably not commit many sins, and we shall do many good things, being fair and moderate; and shall enjoy all the promises to them that love God: to which may all attain, by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost, be glory, power, honor, now and for ever and world with end.
